name: God Object
description: A class that knows too much or does too much, violating the single responsibility principle.
symptoms: One class with hundreds or thousands of lines
Class that other classes heavily depend on
Difficult to name because it does everything
example_bad:
class Application:
    def __init__(self):
        self.db = Database()
        self.users = []
        self.orders = []
        self.products = []
        self.config = {}
    
    def create_user(self, name, email): ...
    def delete_user(self, id): ...
    def authenticate(self, email, password): ...
    def create_order(self, user_id, items): ...
    def process_payment(self, order_id, payment_info): ...
    def ship_order(self, order_id): ...
    def add_product(self, name, price): ...
    def update_inventory(self, product_id, quantity): ...
    def send_email(self, to, subject, body): ...
    def generate_report(self, type): ...
    def log_event(self, event): ...
    # ... 100 more methods

refactoring_strategies.name: Extract Class
refactoring_strategies.description: Identify related methods and fields, extract into new class

refactoring_strategies.name: Single Responsibility Split
refactoring_strategies.description: Identify distinct responsibilities and create a class for each

refactoring_strategies.name: Facade Introduction
refactoring_strategies.description: Keep god object as facade, delegate to specialized classes

prevention: Set class size limits in linting
Think before adding methods to existing classes
